name: Fort DeRussy Beach Park
tagline: Waikiki's widest, least-crowded beach — BBQ grills, calm water, and actual space to put your towel down.
parking: Paid lot off Kalia Rd ($4/hr); street parking on Saratoga Rd if you're lucky
bestTime: Weekday mornings for a near-private beach experience; weekends for BBQ vibes
talkStory: Fort DeRussy is the beach locals send you to when you say you want to go to Waikiki but hate crowds. It's a 71-acre military park that's almost entirely open to the public. The beach here is wider than the main Waikiki strip and the water is just as calm. There are BBQ grills and picnic tables in the park — one of the only spots in Waikiki where you can actually grill. The U.S. Army Museum inside the old battery is free and worth a quick walk-through. Workout stations along the path if you're that type. The Hale Koa Hotel next door is military-only, but the beach and park are for everyone.
